mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market slack and suppressed consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term positive market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of weeks following INDPRO increases, or the signal reverses direction during inflationary supply bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 260 indicate accelerating inflationary pressures that erode real corporate earnings and prompt anticipatory risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns dominate when CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, or the inflation signal shows zero predictive power (Rank IC = 0) across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue fundamentals, which systematically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters following GDPC1 increases, or the GDP signal exhibits negative Rank IC over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 baseline index of 100 signals persistent manufacturing contraction and supply-side weakness, which triggers short-term risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >50% of weeks when INDPRO remains below 100, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for monetary policy expectations.
